ground_truth.title: How Much Does It Cost To Remodel a House? Here's the Step-By-Step Breakdown
ground_truth.author: Kat Piccolo
ground_truth.main_content:
Making a house a home is no easy feat. Whether you're just moving in or you're trying to rework your space, knowing the basics can feel like opening an encyclopedia — cost to remodel a house vs. renovate, minor cosmetic changes vs. gut jobs, contractor vs. DIY — the list goes on. That's why we're here to break down the basics, including the answer to the age-old question: "How much does it cost to renovate a house?" We've gathered the average costs for common remodeling jobs, answered a few of your most-asked questions, and even left some tips to help you get started.

Whole House Remodel vs. Whole House Renovation: The first question to ask yourself: Are you remodeling or renovating? Renovations involve cosmetic changes to alter the appearance of a home without changing the overall structure. Home remodels do exactly what they say — remodel the existing structure of a home. It can be creating an addition on the outside of your home or adding a shower to your bathroom. Renovations are more for bringing something out of disrepair into a good state, while remodels are about changing the form of a room.

Average Cost to Remodel a House: Minor home remodel: $15,000 - $45,000; Moderate home remodel: $46,000 - $70,000; Whole house remodel: $71,000+. The average cost to renovate a house is slightly higher, with the normal range falling between $19,524 and $87,743.

Average Cost Based on Size: Cost to remodel 1,000 square feet: $10,000 - $60,000; Cost to remodel 1,500 square feet: $15,000 - $90,000; Cost to remodel 2,000 square feet: $20,000 - $120,000.

Kitchen: Range: $14,597 - $41,482; Average: $26,945. No matter your budget, cabinets are likely to take up the majority of it, followed by flooring, countertops, and then appliances and lighting.

Bathroom: Range: $6,634 - $17,594; Average: $12,080. In your bathroom, you're likely to spend the most on plumbing, electrical, and any necessary repairs from water damage or moisture.

Garage: Range: $7,000 - $30,000; Average: $18,000. The average size of a one-car garage is around 250 square feet, while the average size of a four-car garage is around 900.

Bedroom: Range: $4,000 - $40,000; Average: $20,000. A basic bedroom remodel might include new flooring, a coat of paint, and some closet upgrades.

Basement: Range: $12,027 - $34,346; Average: $22,643. A basement remodel can be a confusing task, but that's because there are so many different possibilities for the space.

When You Should Hire vs. When You Should DIY: Choosing to hire a contractor vs. DIYing everything can have a major effect on the overall cost to remodel a house, but some things are simply best left to the professionals: Electrical, Plumbing, Tile work.

Factors That Affect Overall Cost: How Big Is Your Home? The size of your home will have an impact on your budget. How Much Work Do You Need Done? Demolition day comes and you open up the walls to find a host of problems. Unexpected damage, pests, out-of-date electrical work — you'll likely need to bring everything up to code. How Many Times Are You Willing To Say Yes? It's easy to say, "Yeah, go ahead and get that done while you're at it!" but it's smart to place a limit on how many times you're going to "just get it done."

Cost to Remodel a House — FAQs: Is $100,000 enough to renovate a house? Yes, especially when you consider the average for a whole-home remodel starts at $71,000. Is $50,000 enough to renovate a house? It can be enough, but you'll likely find yourself prioritizing certain rooms over others. How much should you spend on a remodel? You shouldn't spend more than 10 to 15 percent of your home's overall value on a single room. What is the most expensive thing when renovating a house? The kitchen is likely to be the most expensive thing. How long does it take to remodel a house? Minor remodels can take anywhere from a few days to a few months, a moderate home remodel can take several weeks to six months, while a whole home remodel can take six months to over a year.

Tips for Remodeling Your Home: 1. Plan Ahead. Have a checklist on hand, establish your budget, and think about where you want to start. 2. Ask for Referrals. The best place to find that support? Your friends, family, and the ever-trusted Google reviews. 3. Consider Return on Investment (ROI). A whole home remodel could bring in a 40 to 80 percent ROI, but the average return on a minor kitchen remodel alone is between 73 and 84 percent. 4. Have Everything On-Site. Make your life easy from the start and plan storage ahead of time.
with: Making a house a home is no easy feat. Whether you're just moving in or you're trying to rework your space, knowing the basics can feel like opening an encyclopedia — cost to remodel a house vs. renovate, minor cosmetic changes vs. gut jobs, contractor vs. DIY — the list goes on.
Renovations involve cosmetic changes to alter the appearance of a home without changing the overall structure. Home remodels do exactly what they say — remodel the existing structure of a home.
Minor home remodel: $15,000 - $45,000; Moderate home remodel: $46,000 - $70,000; Whole house remodel: $71,000+.
No matter your budget, cabinets are likely to take up the majority of it, followed by flooring, countertops, and then appliances and lighting.

The kitchen is likely to be the most expensive thing when renovating a house. The other more expensive renovations include the bathroom, creating home additions, and renovating the entire exterior of your home.
